    max_area (PositiveInt): The maximum area for despeckling.

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: A dictionary containing the job ID.

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: A dictionary containing the job ID.

get.description:
List files in given directory.

Args:
    dir_id (UUID): id of the directory to list

Raises:
    HTTPException: if directory is not found.

Returns:
    list[str]: list of files found under the directory.

schema.title: Response List Directory Api V0 1 Listdir  Dir Id  Get

post.description:
Launch a job to apply operations on all valid image files in a directory.

Args:
    request (ComposeBatchRequest): Directory path and operations list.

Returns:
    JobID: Celery job ID for tracking the background task.

Batch clean images in a directory.

Args:
    item_id (UUID): The ID of the directory to clean.

Returns:

    max_area (PositiveInt): The maximum area for despeckling.

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: A dictionary containing the job ID.

get.description:
Classify document types in the specified directory.

Args:
    uuid (UUID): Unique identifier representing the directory path to classify.

Returns:
    JobID: An object containing the job ID for tracking the classification task.

post.description:
Create file partitions for the given UUID batch.

Starts a job that analyzes files in the directory corresponding to the provided UUID
and creates partitions by identifying end-of-File marker document type (e.g. Topsheet).

Args:
    uuid (UUID): Unique identifier for the file partitioning job.

Returns:
    JobID: Job ID of the created file partitioning task.

Extract metadata from given `paths`, based on `doctype` and `project`.

The response is a
[Streaming Response](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Streams_API),
in `jsonl` format, where each line will contain information for respective input path.

## Example:
If `paths` is `'[
    "e4a19cf1-eac3-4348-af5e-b1ef8eab0c73/abc.TIF",
    "7ae79c02-a31c-4c33-8583-e000fe3bdcde/efg.TIF"
]'`

Then corresponding response will be streamed, and lines will be like:

```
'{"field1": "sdfa", "field2": "123n"}\n'
'{"field1": "ljkd", "field2": "349j"}\n'
```

organizationId.description: Optional. Only include if the project belongs to an organization. Must be a valid MongoDB ObjectId.
